full_response:
We are given the function  
 f(x(λ), y(λ), λ) = λ² + sin[x(λ)] + y(λ)/x(λ).  

We wish to compute  
1. ∂f/∂x  
2. ∂f/∂y  
3. df/dλ  

Step 1.  Identify f as a function of three independent “variables” (x, y, λ):  
 f(x,y,λ) = λ² + sin x + y/x.  

Step 2.  Compute the partial derivatives holding the other “variables” fixed.  

(a) ∂f/∂x:  
 We treat y and λ as constants.  
 • ∂(λ²)/∂x = 0.  
 • ∂(sin x)/∂x = cos x.  
 • ∂(y/x)/∂x = y · ∂(1/x)/∂x = y · (−1/x²) = −y/x².  
Hence  
 ∂f/∂x = cos x − y/x².  

(b) ∂f/∂y:  
 We treat x and λ as constants.  
 • ∂(λ²)/∂y = 0.  
 • ∂(sin x)/∂y = 0.  
 • ∂(y/x)/∂y = (1/x).  
Hence  
 ∂f/∂y = 1/x.  

Step 3.  Compute the total derivative df/dλ using the chain rule:  
 df/dλ = (∂f/∂x)(dx/dλ) + (∂f/∂y)(dy/dλ) + (∂f/∂λ).  

We already have ∂f/∂x and ∂f/∂y.  Also  
 ∂f/∂λ = ∂(λ²)/∂λ + ∂(sin x)/∂λ + ∂(y/x)/∂λ  
  = 2λ + 0 + 0  
  = 2λ.  

Let x′ ≡ dx/dλ and y′ ≡ dy/dλ.  Then  
 df/dλ = (cos x − y/x²)·x′ + (1/x)·y′ + 2λ.  

Boxing the final results:  
\boxed{
\begin{aligned}
\frac{∂f}{∂x} &= \cos x \;-\;\frac{y}{x^2},\\
\frac{∂f}{∂y} &= \frac{1}{x},\\
\frac{df}{dλ} &= \Bigl(\cos x - \frac{y}{x^2}\Bigr)\,\frac{dx}{dλ}
\;+\;\frac{1}{x}\,\frac{dy}{dλ}
\;+\;2λ\,.  
\end{aligned}
}  

Confidence: 100%
